# AGENTS.md — Instructions for LLM Agents

## Project Overview

**Extrator de Fotos de Fichas do Q-Acadêmico** is a client-side web application that extracts student photos and captions from PDF files generated by the Q-Acadêmico system (used by IFCE — Instituto Federal do Ceará, Campus Canindé). All processing happens in the browser — no data is sent to external servers.

**Core workflow:**

1. User loads a PDF containing student photo cards
2. User configures cut parameters (margins, dimensions, spacing, caption area) via sliders
3. Visual guides overlay the PDF preview (orange = photos, yellow = captions)
4. Real-time preview of cropped images and OCR-read captions
5. User clicks "Process" → images are extracted, OCR reads registration numbers, files are named accordingly
6. A ZIP file is generated for download

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Framework | Nuxt 4 (Vue 3) |
| UI Library | PrimeVue 4 |
| Styling | TailwindCSS 4 |
| Language | TypeScript |
| PDF Rendering | pdfjs-dist |
| OCR | Tesseract.js |
| ZIP Creation | JSZip |
| Icons | @nuxt/icon + @iconify-json/material-symbols |
| Utilities | @vueuse/core |
| Package Manager | pnpm |
| Linting | @nuxt/eslint + eslint-config-prettier |
| Formatting | Prettier (no semicolons, single quotes, no trailing commas) |

---

## Project Structure

```
app/
├── app.vue                    # Root Vue component
├── assets/css/main.css        # Tailwind CSS entry
├── composables/
│   └── useCutProfiles.ts      # Profile management composable (localStorage persistence)
├── components/
│   ├── AppHeader.vue           # Header with title and dark mode toggle
│   ├── AppFooter.vue           # Footer with credits
│   ├── CutProperties.vue       # Sliders + profile dropdown for cut configuration
│   ├── DarkModeToggler.vue     # Dark mode toggle button
│   ├── DownloadFiles.vue       # Process button and ZIP download
│   ├── LoadPDF.vue             # PDF file upload component
│   ├── PdfView.vue             # PDF preview with canvas overlay for cut guides
│   └── PreviewCut.vue          # Preview of cropped image and OCR text
├── pages/
│   └── index.vue               # Main page (instructions + PDF loading + profile state)
└── utils/
    ├── index.ts                # Utility functions (PDF conversion, OCR, ZIP)
    └── types.ts                # TypeScript type definitions and defaults
```

---

## How to Run

```bash
pnpm install          # Install dependencies
pnpm dev              # Dev server at http://localhost:3000
pnpm build            # Production build
pnpm preview           # Preview production build
```

Deployment is automatic via GitHub Actions on push to `main` (GitHub Pages).

---

## Code Conventions

### Vue 3 Composition API

- Use `<script setup lang="ts">` in all components
- Use `defineProps<T>()`, `defineEmits<T>()`, and `defineModel<T>()` with TypeScript generics
- Two-way binding via `defineModel` (Vue 3.5+ pattern)
- Reactive state with `ref<T>()` and `computed()`

### TypeScript

- All types are defined in `app/utils/types.ts`
- Key types: `CutConfig`, `CutProfile`, `Rect`, `CutRects`, `PDFPages`
- Cut configuration values are **normalized** (0–1 range, proportions of page dimensions) — never absolute pixel values
- The `DEFAULT_CUT_CONFIG` constant provides sensible defaults
- The `DEFAULT_PROFILE_NAME` constant is `'Q-Acadêmico'` — the built-in default profile
- The `DEFAULT_PROFILES` array contains the immutable built-in profiles (`Q-Acadêmico` and `SUAP`)
- The `DEFAULT_PROFILE_NAMES` array lists all default profile names (used to prevent modification/deletion of built-in profiles)

### Client-Side Only

- Many utility functions check `import.meta.client` before executing browser-only code (PDF.js, canvas, OCR)
- Always guard browser APIs with `if (!import.meta.client) return` when adding new utilities

### Performance

- `refDebounced` (VueUse, 1000ms) is used for cut rect calculations to avoid excessive re-renders during slider adjustments
- `PdfView.vue` uses `CANVAS_RATIO = 0.25` — the overlay canvas is 1/4 size for performance; coordinates are scaled up when extracting actual regions

### Styling

- TailwindCSS utility classes for layout and spacing
- PrimeVue components for interactive UI elements (sliders, buttons, dialogs)
- Dark mode is supported via `DarkModeToggler.vue`

### Formatting (Prettier)

- No semicolons
- Single quotes
- No trailing commas
- Run `pnpm format` to auto-format

### Linting

- ESLint with `@nuxt/eslint` + `eslint-config-prettier`
- Run `pnpm lint` to check

---

## Brazilian Portuguese Text Rules

**This is critical.** The application serves Brazilian Portuguese users. All user-facing strings must use correct Portuguese orthography with proper diacritics (accents).

### Correct examples

- "Máquina" (not "Maquina" missing the accent — always use `á`, `é`, `í`, `ó`, `ú`, `ã`, `õ`, `â`, `ê`, `ô`, `ç`)
- "Espaçamento Horizontal" (not "Espacamento Horizontal")
- "Nenhuma imagem válida encontrada" (not "valida" — `á` not `a`)
- "Processadas X imagens"
- "Carregar PDF"
- "Configurações de Corte"
- "Visualização"

### When adding or editing text

- **UI labels, buttons, messages, tooltips, placeholders** → always in Brazilian Portuguese with correct diacritics
- **Code comments** → may be in Portuguese or English, but if Portuguese, use correct diacritics
- **Variable/function names** → English (standard programming convention)
- **AGENTS.md, README.md, technical docs** → English is acceptable
- **Commit messages** → English preferred

### Common Portuguese words in this project

| Word | Correct | Wrong |
|------|---------|-------|
| left margin | Margem Esquerda | Margem Esquerda |
| spacing | Espaçamento | Espacamento |
| caption/legend | Legenda | Legenda |
| configuration | Configuração | Configuracao |
| visualization | Visualização | Visualizacao |
| valid | válida | valida |
| number | número | numero |
| registration | matrícula | matricula |
| file | arquivo | arquivo |
| download | baixar | baixar |
| processing | processamento | processamento |
| image | imagem | imagem |
| photo | foto | foto |
| student | estudante | estudante |

---

## Key Architectural Decisions

1. **No backend** — everything runs client-side for privacy and simplicity
2. **Normalized coordinates** — cut parameters are proportions (0–1), making them DPI-independent
3. **OCR for naming** — Tesseract.js reads student registration numbers from caption areas; images are named `${parseInt(ocrText)}.jpg`
4. **Validation** — `isValidNumber()` checks that OCR text parses to a number > 1000 (typical Brazilian student registration IDs)
5. **PrimeVue locale** — uses `primelocale/pt-BR.json` for localized component labels
6. **Profile system** — cut configurations are managed via named profiles stored in localStorage; the default "Q-Acadêmico" profile is immutable; custom profiles can be saved/deleted by the user
7. **Config persistence** — the selected profile and custom profiles persist across sessions via localStorage; cut config is NOT reset when a new PDF is loaded

---

## Common Tasks

### Adding a new cut parameter

1. Add the property to the `CutConfig` type in `app/utils/types.ts`
2. Add a default value to `DEFAULT_CUT_CONFIG`
3. Add a slider in `CutProperties.vue` using PrimeVue `Slider` component
4. Update `calculateCutRects()` in `app/utils/index.ts` to use the new parameter
5. Update `PdfView.vue` overlay rendering if the parameter affects visual guides

### Working with the profile system

- The `useCutProfiles()` composable (`app/composables/useCutProfiles.ts`) manages profile state and localStorage persistence
- It is provided via `provide(PROFILE_INJECTION_KEY, ...)` in `index.vue` and injected in `CutProperties.vue`
- Default profiles "Q-Acadêmico" and "SUAP" are immutable — cannot be renamed, modified, or deleted
- Custom profiles are stored in localStorage under the key `'cut-profiles'`
- When a profile is selected, `cut_config` in `index.vue` is updated to match the profile's config
- When sliders are modified, the dropdown shows "Personalizado" and action buttons appear contextually:
  - **Salvar** appears when the current config differs from the selected profile
  - **Renomear** and **Excluir** appear only for custom (non-default) profiles
- The `isModified` computed uses `JSON.stringify` with rounding to handle floating-point precision
- `CutProperties.vue` uses `v-for` with a `sliderConfigs` array to render sliders with `InputNumber` fields
- Slider values are stored as integers (1000× normalized) and converted via `multiplyObject`

### Adding a new component

1. Create `app/components/NewComponent.vue` with `<script setup lang="ts">`
2. Use Composition API patterns (see existing components)
3. Use PrimeVue components for interactive elements
4. Use TailwindCSS for layout
5. Ensure dark mode compatibility

### Modifying OCR behavior

- OCR logic is in `app/utils/index.ts` — look for `recognize()` calls
- The `isValidNumber()` function validates OCR output
- Tesseract.js language is configured for Portuguese text recognition
- Always test with real Q-Acadêmico PDFs to ensure registration numbers are read correctly

---

## Testing

Currently no automated tests are configured. When adding tests:

- Prefer Vitest (compatible with Nuxt/Vue ecosystem)
- Test utility functions in `app/utils/` independently
- Mock browser APIs (`canvas`, `FileReader`, `URL.createObjectURL`) in unit tests
- Test OCR validation logic (`isValidNumber`) with edge cases

---

## Deployment

- Automatic via GitHub Actions on push to `main`
- Deploys to GitHub Pages: `https://ifce-campus-caninde.github.io/extracao-fichas-qacademico/`
- Base URL is configured in `nuxt.config.ts`
- No server-side rendering required — the app is fully static (`nuxt generate`)
